div.h2: SHA-256 Working Variable State Machine: 64-Round Evolution ('abc' Block)
div.p: Each round: only a and e receive computed values. All others shift down by one position.

text.tspan: ROUNDS t=1..15 (W FROM PARSED BLOCK WORDS)
ROUNDS t=16..63 (W FROM σ RECURRENCE EXPANSION)

g.text: WORKING VARIABLE ROLES (CONSTANT ACROSS ALL 64 ROUNDS)
READ-AFTER-WRITE SAFETY: ASSIGNMENT ORDER IS LOAD-BEARING
● INITIAL STATE — H[i] loaded from SHA256_INITIAL_HASH (sqrt of primes)
● COMPUTED INJECTION — a = T1+T2, e = d+T1 (only two variables with new values)
● SHIFT ONLY — b←a, c←b, d←c, f←e, g←f, h←g (six variables just propagate)
● FINAL PRE-UPDATE — a..h values after round 63, before H[i] += step

div.p: Step 2 of sha256_compress(): Copy H[0..7] → working variables a..h Source: SHA256_INITIAL_HASH (frac(√prime) × 2³²)

Rounds 1–15: W[t] values come directly from block byte parsing (M2 Phase A). For the 'abc' block: W[1]..W[14] = 0x00000000 (all-zero padding bytes). W[15] = 0x00000018 (bit-length field = 24 bits, big-endian). Pattern per round t:

ul.li: T1 = h_old + Σ1(e_old) + Ch(e_old, f_old, g_old) + K[t] + W[t]
a_new = T1 + T2 ← ONLY injection point for 'a'
e_new = d_old + T1 ← ONLY injection point for 'e'

All others: b=a_old, c=b_old, d=c_old, f=e_old, g=f_old, h=g_old State progresses through 8-deep shift register: The 8 variables form a circular pipeline. After round t, the message data from W[t] has been mixed into 'a' and 'e'. By round 8, every variable has been touched by at least one injection.

g.text: INVARIANT: b←a, c←b, d←c, f←e, g←f, h←g (every round, no exceptions)

div.p: Rounds 16–63: W[t] = σ1(W[t-2]) + W[t-7] + σ0(W[t-15]) + W[t-16] mod 2³² For 'abc': W[16] = 0x61626380, W[17] = 0x000F0000, ... (see M2 schedule output) By round 16: W[16] re-injects the original message word 0x61626380 into the computation — the message data circulates through the schedule expansion. Diffusion: by round 48, every W[t] depends on every original W[0..15] word. Every round's 'a' and 'e' values carry the accumulated influence of all prior message words through the T1/T2 computation. K constants: K[16]..K[63] ensure each round behaves differently even if W values repeat. K[t] = floor(frac(∛prime_t) × 2³²) — "nothing up my sleeve."

g.text: After 64 rounds: every output bit depends on every input bit (avalanche achieved)
For SHA-256('abc'): these are the working variable values after round 63, before H[i] += step

div.p: H[i] += working_var[i] (mod 2³²) for i = 0..7 H[0] += a H[1] += b H[2] += c H[3] += d H[4] += e H[5] += f H[6] += g H[7] += h WHY += and NOT =: The additive fold is the Davies-Meyer feed-forward. Without it, an attacker controlling the last block controls the entire hash. With +=, every prior block's H_old[i] is permanently baked in — changing any block changes H_old, which cascades into every subsequent H value. THE SINGLE MOST IMPACTFUL CHARACTER IN THIS MODULE: '+' in '+='

g.text: FOR SHA-256('abc') — FINAL H[0..7] (= NIST test vector output):
T1 = h + Σ1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t]

div.p: T1 = h + Σ1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t]; ← uses OLD a..h T2 = Σ0(a) + Maj(a,b,c); ← uses OLD a..h h = g; g = f; f = e; ← shift: reads old, writes new e = d + T1; ← injection: e only, NOT d+T1+T2 d = c; c = b; b = a; ← shift: reads old a before overwrite a = T1 + T2; ← injection: MUST come AFTER b=a
Why T1,T2 computed first: they use OLD values of a..h. C evaluates RHS fully before assignment — safe as written. Never reorder b=a to after a=T1+T2 — b would get the NEW a.

g.text: TRAP: e = d + T1 (NOT d + T1 + T2) — T2 goes only to 'a'
TRAP: a = T1 + T2 (NOT just T2) — T1 carries the message input

text.tspan: → T1 computes to specific 32-bit value (see NIST appendix)
→ T2 computes to specific 32-bit value (see NIST appendix)

div.p: Six variables shift (NO computation): h ← g (old g = 0x1f83d9ab) g ← f (old f = 0x9b05688c) f ← e (old e = 0x510e527f) d ← c (old c = 0x3c6ef372) c ← b (old b = 0xbb67ae85) b ← a (old a = 0x6a09e667)

text.tspan: (carries message data W[0] and structure T2)
NOTE: e = d + T1 only — NOT d + T1 + T2

g.text: Ch(e,f,g) = (e&f)^(~e&g) — nonlinear mux: e selects f or g
K[t] — round constant (cube root of t-th prime, frac × 2³²)
W[t] — message schedule word (MESSAGE DATA ENTRY POINT)
T2 has NO message data — structural diffusion only
